title: Calliandra michelii
taxonomy: Calliandra michelii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Calliandra, which consists of approximately 150 species. The plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions. Within the Fabaceae family, Calliandra michelii is part of the subfamily Faboideae. Calliandra michelii is also known as the pink powderpuff.
uses: Calliandra michelii, a species native to Central America, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. Its bark, leaves, and stems are used to make tea or infusions that can help alleviate fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's extracts are also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing and skin conditions. In traditional medicine, Calliandra michelii is used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal uses and their effectiveness.

intro: Calliandra michelii, also known as Pink Powder Puff or Butterfly Tree, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its showy pink flowers and feathery foliage. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Calliandra michelii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Calliandra michelii is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat colds, coughs, and bronchial infections.
